reasoning: The informal statement says a is algebraic over ℚ of degree at most 80, given that a satisfies p(a)=0 for the specific p. The Lean statement encodes this as the existence of a nonzero polynomial q ∈ ℚ[X] with q.natDegree ≤ 80 satisfying aeval a q = 0. This is logically equivalent to "a is algebraic over ℚ with minimal polynomial of degree ≤ 80": such a q exists iff the minimal polynomial of a has degree ≤ 80 (forward: take q = min poly; backward: min poly divides q). The natDegree (rather than degree) convention is safe here because q ≠ 0 is explicitly required. No hidden added/dropped hypotheses — fully faithful.
